negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing separate pinned account and storage caches for pinned system contract reads/inserts, and missing distinction where non-pinned addresses continue using regular TTL/idle caches.
related_but_insufficient_code: execution-cache/src/cached_state.rs uses a single FixedCache for account_cache and storage_cache plus a code_cache. ExecutionCache::new builds capacity-limited caches and contains no pinned account/storage caches or TTL/idle policy.
engine/tree/src/tree/precompile_cache.rs uses moka CacheBuilder with LRU for precompile outputs, not pinned contract account/storage reads.
Searches for pinned/system-contract cache handling in the port only found unrelated comments and no separate pinned cache implementation.

notes: Confirmed gap: current execution cache is capacity/epoch based and not split into pinned vs non-pinned TTL caches.
rationale: Although this is cache plumbing rather than scheduler-specific pool accounting, the candidate directly states a current invariant for pinned system contract reads.
statement: At current tip, pinned contract account and storage reads/inserts must use separate pinned caches without time_to_live or time_to_idle, while non-pinned addresses continue using regular TTL/idle caches.
suggested_falsification_tests: Configure one pinned and one non-pinned contract, read account and storage for both, advance beyond the regular cache idle time, and assert pinned entries remain in pinned caches while non-pinned entries follow regular expiry behavior.
summary: Pinned contract account/storage cache behavior is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Configure one pinned and one non-pinned contract and advance beyond the regular cache idle time; current port has no pinned cache configuration or TTL/idle policy to make this behavior observable.
title: Pinned contract cache entries do not TTL-expire
